name: El Salvador
excerpt: El Salvador achieved independence from Spain in 1821 and from the Central American Federation in 1839.

quick_facts.religions: Roman Catholic 43.9%, Protestant 39.6% (Evangelical - unspecified 38.2%, Evangelical - Methodist 1.3%, Evangelical - Baptist 0.1%), none 16.3%, unspecified 0.2% (2023 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Mestizo 86.3%, White 12.7%, Indigenous 0.2% (includes Lenca, Kakawira, Nahua-Pipil), Black 0.1%, other 0.6% (2007 est.)
quick_facts.climate: tropical; rainy season (May to October); dry season (November to April); tropical on coast; temperate in uplands
quick_facts.terrain: mostly mountains with narrow coastal belt and central plateau
quick_facts.natural_resources: hydropower, geothermal power, petroleum, arable land
quick_facts.major_exports: garments, plastic products, electrical capacitors, raw sugar, toilet paper (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: smallest Central American country and only one without a coastline on the Caribbean Sea
summaries.history_summary: El Salvador achieved independence from Spain in 1821 and from the Central American Federation in 1839. A 12-year civil war, which cost about 75,000 lives, was brought to a close in 1992 when the government and leftist rebels signed a treaty that provided for military and political reforms. El Salvador is beset by one of the world's highest homicide rates and pervasive criminal gangs.
summaries.economy_summary: upper-middle-income, dollarized Central American economy; reliant on remittances from US; recent growth linked to infrastructure investment, consumption, and crime reduction; $1.3 billion IMF loan to address fiscal imbalances; Bitcoin adopted as legal tender; persistent poverty and large informal sector

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: El Salvador has a substantial evangelical population nearly equal to its Catholic majority. Pray for this movement to be marked by sound biblical doctrine and genuine discipleship.
prayer_points: Pray for the continued strengthening of Bible-believing, autonomous local churches across El Salvador.
Pray for pastors to be trained in expository preaching.
Pray for Salvadorans practicing nominal Catholicism to hear the clear gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: El Salvador's substantial evangelical population is real ground to build on alongside the Catholic majority. Ongoing needs include deepening biblical literacy among existing churches and reaching nominal Catholics with the clear gospel.
